rationale: The cited paper is used in a specific methodological context, not just a generic literature list. In the model specification section, the authors cite Van Heerde, Leeflang, and Wittink (2000) to justify that lagged promotion effects can be positive due to repeat purchases or in-store implementation inertia, which motivates inclusion and interpretation of lagged promotion terms. Although the citation is limited in frequency, it has a clear functional role in the conceptual setup of promotion dynamics in the citing study.
relation_description: The citing paper draws on Van Heerde et al. (2000) as prior empirical evidence about postpromotion dynamics to motivate its lagged-promotion specification and interpretation. It uses that insight as input to model design rather than directly extending the 2000 model.
